title: DelegationResult
description: The ONE shape the exit gate (STORY-033.6) consumes regardless of which external CLI or driver (headless/acp) produced it. Authority is explicit: `diff` (git diff vs the PRE-delegation tree) is AUTHORITATIVE and is what gets gated; `claimed_changes` and `agent_self_report` are ADVISORY (diagnosis only) and NEVER gated. A missing/malformed self-report never blocks the gate because the diff is the source of truth. Lie-detection: when claimed_changes != the diff's file set, a warning is recorded — it is NOT a gate failure. Design: docs/architecture/17_EXTERNAL_AGENT_DELEGATION.md.

cli.description: Which external CLI produced the run.

driver.description: Which driver drove it. The result shape is identical for both.

diff.description: AUTHORITATIVE. git diff of the sandbox working tree vs the recorded pre-delegation tree. This — and only this — is what the exit gate enforces against the write-set.

killed.description: True if the entry gate killed the run (wall-clock/budget). Optional.

kill_reason.description: Set only when killed is true.

claimed_changes.description: ADVISORY self-report — file paths the agent CLAIMS it changed. Never gated. Used only for lie-detection vs the authoritative diff.

agent_self_report.description: ADVISORY — the agent's structured self-evaluation, for diagnosis only. Never gated. Acquired via native schema (Claude --json-schema / Codex --output-schema) or validated-on-receipt (Gemini); dropped if non-conforming.

self_report_source.description: How the self-report was acquired/handled. native_schema = the CLI guaranteed our schema (Claude/Codex); validated_on_receipt = prompted + validated (Gemini); dropped_nonconforming = received but failed validation (flagged, not trusted); none = no self-report.

warnings.description: Non-fatal signals (e.g. lie-detection: claimed_changes != diff file set). Recorded for honesty signal; NEVER a gate failure.
